description: Implement a DMS-1055 GET-many authorization strategy classifier for the relational read path. Acceptance: `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly` and `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted` are classified as supported relationship strategies; `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` is classified as a no-op; `NamespaceBased`, `OwnershipBased`, all People relationship strategies, and valid `{BasisResource}With...` custom view-based names that resolve to a known resource or descriptor are classified as known-but-not-implemented for GET-many; unknown strategy names, invalid custom view names, and custom view names with unresolved basis resources are classified as security configuration failures. Classification is deterministic and preserves the effective strategy names needed for diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for supported, no-op, known unsupported, convention-matching custom view, unresolved custom view, and unknown strategy names.

description: Add constants and shared registration support for `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted`. Acceptance: the strategy name is available through `AuthorizationStrategyNameConstants`; the authorization filter/provider registry recognizes it; GET-many can carry the strategy name into the relational backend; GET-by-id, POST, PUT, and DELETE return an explicit not-implemented result until DMS-1056 rather than falling through to missing-provider 403 behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add core unit tests proving inverted strategy provider lookup succeeds and non-GET operations fail intentionally as not implemented.

description: Introduce a typed request-scoped relational authorization context for GET-many. Acceptance: the context is built from `ClientAuthorizations`, carries `ClaimEducationOrganizationIds` as unique sorted `long` values, and is passed through `RelationalQueryRequest`/`IRelationalQueryRequest` to `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository`; relational GET-many no longer parses EdOrg IDs from `AuthorizationStrategyEvaluator.Filters`; duplicate token EdOrg IDs are removed before SQL generation, threshold selection, logging, and binding.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for null/empty, duplicate, unsorted, and already-sorted EdOrg claim lists.

description: Refactor the current authorization filter middleware enough for relational GET-many to reach backend planning with empty EdOrg claims, inverted EdOrg-only strategy names, and known unsupported mixed strategies. Acceptance: relational GET-many does not receive the legacy empty-EdOrg 403 from `AuthorizationFiltersProviderBase.GetRelationshipFilters`; write and single-item paths keep their existing behavior until their implementation stories; the backend, not the middleware, decides between successful filtering, empty page, 501 known-not-implemented, and 500 security configuration failure.
steps-to-verify: Add core middleware/handler tests for relational GET-many with empty EdOrg claims, inverted strategy, unsupported known strategy, and `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` mixed with an EdOrg relationship strategy.

description: Add the minimal request-time security configuration failure result and response mapping needed by DMS-1055. Acceptance: invalid/missing security metadata, unknown strategy names, unresolvable EdOrg securable paths, and EdOrg relationship strategies with no applicable EdOrg securable elements return HTTP 500 with type `urn:ed-fi:api:system-configuration:security`, title `Security Configuration Error`, detail `A security configuration problem was detected. The request cannot be authorized.`, a correlationId, and logged diagnostic details; normal GET-many denials still return 200 filtered results; known-but-not-implemented strategies still return 501.
steps-to-verify: Add response factory and query handler tests for the canonical security configuration ProblemDetails shape.

description: Extend the resolved securable element path contract used by mapping sets so each resolved path carries the original JSON path and readable MetaEd name when available. Acceptance: EdOrg paths expose enough metadata for diagnostics and per-element authorization; root-table and child-table EdOrg locations remain represented by a single `ColumnPathStep` with null target table/column; existing mapping set and normalized plan contract tests are updated for the new shape without changing path resolution semantics.
steps-to-verify: Add/adjust unit tests in the securable element column path resolver for EdOrg root paths, child collection paths, and multiple EdOrg paths with distinct readable names.

description: Extend the page-query parameter model and hydration binder to support authorization list parameters. Acceptance: PostgreSQL uses one bigint array parameter named `ClaimEducationOrganizationIds`; SQL Server uses expanded scalar bigint parameters named `ClaimEducationOrganizationIds_0`, `ClaimEducationOrganizationIds_1`, etc. for fewer than 2,000 unique IDs; SQL Server uses one structured parameter named `ClaimEducationOrganizationIds` with type `dms.BigIntTable` and one `Id` column for 2,000 or more unique IDs; provider-specific binding metadata stays out of compiled/AOT plan delegates and is applied in the runtime executor/binder layer.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for PostgreSQL array metadata, SQL Server 1,999 scalar parameters, SQL Server 2,000 TVP metadata, and duplicate IDs being deduped before threshold selection.

description: Implement authorization SQL generation for EdOrg securable paths that resolve to the root resource table. Acceptance: `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly` emits an `IN`/`ANY` subquery against `auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId` with token IDs matched to `SourceEducationOrganizationId` and the resource column matched to `TargetEducationOrganizationId`; `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted` swaps Source and Target; multiple EdOrg elements within one strategy are combined with AND; multiple supported relationship strategies are combined with OR; `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` contributes no predicate; generated SQL uses subqueries rather than joins so duplicate auth rows cannot duplicate root documents.
steps-to-verify: Add SQL shape tests for normal, inverted, normal plus inverted OR, multiple EdOrg elements AND, no-op `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired`, and duplicate-avoidance `IN` subquery structure.

description: Implement authorization SQL generation for EdOrg securable paths that resolve to child collection tables. Acceptance: root `DocumentId`s are filtered through child-table predicates; for each configured child-table EdOrg path, a document is authorized only when at least one child row exists for that path and no existing child row for that path is unauthorized; documents with no child rows for that configured path are excluded; normal and inverted Source/Target semantics match the root-table implementation; multiple child/root EdOrg elements within one strategy are combined with AND.
steps-to-verify: Add SQL shape tests that verify the `EXISTS` plus `NOT EXISTS` child-row semantics for normal and inverted strategies.
Add an execution-focused unit or integration fixture where one unauthorized child row excludes the whole document and a document with no child rows for the path is excluded.

description: Integrate EdOrg authorization predicates into relational GET-many page keyset planning. Acceptance: authorization filters are applied in the same page `DocumentId` query as normal query predicates; `TotalCountSql` counts only authorized rows; offset/limit are applied after authorization and query predicates; existing descriptor/document UUID query preprocessing still works; generated authorization SQL is not cached separately, while resolved securable paths from the mapping set are reused.
steps-to-verify: Add planner/compiler tests for authorization plus API query filters, authorization plus totalCount, and pagination after authorization.

description: Replace the current relational read guardrail for GET-many with DMS-1055 outcomes. Acceptance: classify strategies before checking empty EdOrg claims; known unsupported mixed strategies return `QueryFailureNotImplemented`/HTTP 501; supported EdOrg relationship strategies with an empty unique EdOrg list return `QuerySuccess` with an empty page and Total-Count 0 when requested; `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` alone still returns unfiltered results; `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` mixed with EdOrg relationship strategies is ignored as a no-op while EdOrg auth applies; security configuration failures use the new 500 result path.
steps-to-verify: Add repository unit tests for each outcome, including the ordering of unsupported-strategy 501 before empty-claims short-circuit.

description: Add PostgreSQL backend integration coverage for EdOrg-only relationship GET-many authorization. Acceptance: normal EdOrg filtering returns only reachable resources; inverted filtering returns bottom-to-top authorized resources; normal plus inverted strategies are ORed; multiple EdOrg securable elements in one strategy are ANDed; duplicate auth rows or multiple matching strategies return each document once; totalCount and pagination are based on the authorized row set; empty EdOrg claims return an empty page and Total-Count 0 when requested; unresolvable/no EdOrg securable paths fail as security configuration errors.
steps-to-verify: Add focused tests to the PostgreSQL relational backend integration suite using provisioned auth hierarchy data.

description: Add SQL Server backend integration coverage for EdOrg-only relationship GET-many authorization and provider-specific parameter binding. Acceptance: normal, inverted, OR, AND, duplicate avoidance, pagination, totalCount, empty claims, and security configuration scenarios match PostgreSQL behavior; 1,999 unique EdOrg IDs bind as expanded scalar parameters; 2,000 unique EdOrg IDs bind as a `dms.BigIntTable` TVP with an `Id` column; duplicate token IDs are deduped before threshold selection.
steps-to-verify: Add focused tests to the SQL Server relational backend integration suite, including assertions on scalar-vs-TVP binding behavior.

description: Add temporary DMS-1055 test coverage for unsupported mixed strategies. Acceptance: GET-many with `NamespaceBased`, `OwnershipBased`, People relationship strategies, or valid convention-matching custom view-based strategies returns HTTP 501 from the relational path; unknown built-in-looking names, invalid custom view names, and custom view names whose basis resource cannot resolve return the security configuration 500 shape; these tests are named/commented so later stories can update them to the final `auth.md` composition semantics.
steps-to-verify: Add backend and core handler tests covering each unsupported/unknown category.

description: Add permanent test-only CMS/DMS claim set artifacts for `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted`. Acceptance: a committed `E2E-RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInvertedClaimSet` fixture is available to the DMS E2E setup without per-test metadata mutation; existing `E2E-RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyClaimSet` behavior is not changed; fixture naming makes the DMS-1055 coverage easy to find and maintain.
steps-to-verify: Run CMS/DMS fixture setup tests or the smallest claim-set metadata test available.

description: Add focused Data Management Service E2E tests for the DMS-1055 runtime behavior through real token and claim-set wiring. Acceptance: E2E verifies normal EdOrg filtering, inverted filtering, normal plus inverted OR semantics, pagination/Total-Count after authorization, empty EdOrg claims returning 200 with an empty page and Total-Count 0, and a known unsupported mixed strategy returning 501; tests run against the `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E` Docker stack.

Run formatting and the focused verification suite for the completed DMS-1055 implementation. Acceptance: changed C# files are formatted with CSharpier; core unit, backend unit, plans unit, PostgreSQL integration, SQL Server integration, and focused DMS E2E tests pass or any environment-only failures are documented with logs; `tasks.json` remains valid JSON and all task `completed` flags stay false until tasks are actually implemented.
steps-to-verify: Run the focused PostgreSQL, SQL Server, and DMS E2E authorization tests added by earlier tasks.
